description_long: This object is a small, vintage-style biplane with a compact fuselage and two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically. The wings and tail surfaces are painted in a muted orange color with blue tips, while the fuselage and undercarriage are a darker brown and black. The airplane features a single wooden propeller at the nose, mounted in front of a rounded engine cowling, and a visible machine gun mounted above the upper wing, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The landing gear consists of two large, fixed wheels and a small tail skid, supporting the aircraft in a slightly nose-up attitude. The tail section includes a conventional vertical stabilizer and rudder, with a horizontal stabilizer and elevator mounted low on the tail. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with no visible canopy or enclosure.
description: A small, vintage biplane with orange and blue wings, a wooden propeller, and an open cockpit behind the upper wing. It features fixed landing gear and a conventional tail design.
description_view_0: The left-front perspective highlights the stacked biplane wings, the wooden propeller, and the open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: The top-down view shows the full wingspan, the blue-tipped orange wings, and the symmetrical tail surfaces.
description_view_2: The right-front perspective reveals the machine gun above the upper wing, the fixed landing gear, and the tail skid.
description_view_3: The direct front view emphasizes the propeller, the rounded engine cowling, and the alignment of the biplane wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and low-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage, with no canopy.
